It's a dungeon crawler but everything is made of blocks and a giant square cow is trying to kill me.
I suck up adorable bouncing blobs with a vacuum cleaner, feed them carrots, and sell their poop for money.
I have 22 minutes to explore a solar system before the sun explodes. I spent the last loop roasting a marshmallow.
I walked across a beautiful desert with a random stranger, chirped at them a few times, and cried when we parted ways.
A married couple on the brink of divorce gets turned into dolls and forced to cooperate by a talking book with a terrible accent.
Broke out of prison with a buddy. We had to perfectly time our button presses to distract guards and eventually cried at the ending.
An incredibly lucky man climbs crumbling cliffs, destroys ancient ruins, and murders hundreds of mercenaries to find pirate treasure.
I played the guitar, got incredibly depressed, and debated the cycle of revenge while sneaking past clickers.
I shoot arrows at giant robotic dinosaurs in a post-apocalyptic world. The lore is amazing, but fighting the robot T-Rex is terrifying.
I parried a giant ape, cut off its head, and then it got back up and picked up its own head. I need therapy.
Blindfolded androids fighting robots. You have to beat the game three times to understand the story, and the end deletes your save file.
I ride a giant dog around a village and swing an oversized sword at a wyvern so I can make a hat out of its scales.
Kids who are also squids shoot colorful ink at each other in intense turf wars. The fashion is incredible.
A pink puffball inhales a car, a vending machine, and a traffic cone to save a post-apocalyptic world.
I spent an hour in virtual reality trying to juggle headcrabs and drawing on a dirty window with a marker.
